attacking_style: Attacks on penultimate climbs or 3-10km from summit, often from breakaways rather than head-to-head with GC elites. Prefers distance efforts over explosive accelerations, showing pattern of attacking too early on short stages.
team_tactics: Limited team support visible; often relies on breakaway positioning rather than domestique-led tempo control. DSM/AG2R squads show TTT weakness costing GC time. Frequently works in domestique role himself when not protected leader.
terrain_preference: Long alpine climbs where sustained power matters more than explosive accelerations. Thrives in breakaway scenarios on high-altitude ascents (Galibier). Struggles on steep, punchy finishes requiring repeated accelerations and all time trial formats.
vulnerability: Time trials consistently cost 50+ seconds to 2+ minutes regardless of distance. Cannot follow when top GC climbers accelerate hard. Mechanicals and crashes disproportionately derail campaigns (2021 Vuelta crash, 2018 cobbles, 2021 chain drop). Weak team support in crucial moments.
key_patterns: Wins and KOM jerseys earned from breakaways, not head-to-head climbing superiority (2019 TdF, 2017 races)
Time trial weakness persists across all years: lost 50s-2min repeatedly in ITTs/TTTs (2017-2018)
Attacks neutralized easily by elite GC rivals when racing head-to-head (THOMAS/YATES 2018, QUINTANA 2019)
Multiple Tour podiums but GC collapses evident: 4th to 9th in 2022 TdF without mechanical
KOM jersey strategy relies on breakaway persistence across flat stages, not mountain dominance
Form peaks show aggressive late-stage attacking but lacks acceleration to distance marked rivals (2023 Suisse)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed renewed aggression (Tour de Suisse) but tactical errors persist (Vuelta mistimed attack). GC ambitions appear abandoned post-2022 collapse, focusing on stage hunting and KOM competition instead.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
